channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Vincent Osiemo Dies After Falling From Vehicle During Political Rally in Kisii; Family Demands Justice
item.description: Vincent Osiemo, known as Mapinduzi, died after falling from a moving vehicle during a political rally in Kisii. The incident occurred when attackers threw stones at the convoy, forcing vehicles to spe...

item.title: EAC Kiswahili Commission Discusses AI's Role in Language Development
item.description: The third International Kiswahili Conference, held in Burundi from July 5, 2026, focused on integrating artificial intelligence (AI) to advance the Kiswahili language across East Africa. Dr. Caroline ...

item.title: Mumbai Monsoon Tragedy: Man Killed After Tree Falls on Shop, City Under Red Alert
item.description: Mumbai faced severe monsoon rains on Sunday, leading to tragic incidents and widespread disruptions. A 63-year-old man died after a tree collapsed on his shop in Kurla West, trapping him inside. The i...

item.title: Canadian swimmer Summer McIntosh sets 200m butterfly world record at trials
item.description: At the Canadian swimming trials in Montreal, 19-year-old phenom Summer McIntosh shattered the women’s 200-metre butterfly world record with a time of 2:01.65, breaking the previous mark set by Chinese...

item.title: Long-Short Strategies Offer Tax Efficiency Benefits for Investors
item.description: Long-short strategies, which combine long positions in stocks with short positions, provide tax efficiency benefits by offsetting capital gains with realized losses. This approach, explained by Jeff B...

item.title: Morocco advances in World Cup after decisive victory over Canada
item.description: Morocco's midfield maestro Azzedine Ounahi delivered a standout performance in their 3-0 World Cup round-of-16 victory over Canada, capitalizing on a tactical shift that placed him higher up the pitch...

item.title: UK Government Proposes Donation Caps for Overseas Voters and Stricter Company Donation Checks
item.description: The UK government has proposed new restrictions on political donations from overseas voters relocating to the country, including a £100,000 annual cap. These measures aim to prevent foreign influence ...

item.title: Tanzania's Religious Leader Announces Private Militia Amid Political Tensions
item.description: Sheikh Said Mwaipopo, a prominent Muslim cleric in Tanzania, has sparked intense debate by announcing his private militia, 'Black Tiger,' to combat anti-government protests. The group, composed of you...

item.title: NRC Proposes Eliminating ALARA Radiation Standard to Cut Costs
item.description: The U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) has proposed eliminating the ALARA (As Low as Reasonably Achievable) radiation protection standard, a move that could allow higher radiation exposure for w...

item.title: Super Typhoon Inday Expected to Enter PAR Without Making Landfall
item.description: The Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (PAGASA) has issued updates on the potential path of a developing super typhoon, which is expected to enter the Philipp...

item.title: FUOYE Deputy Dean Disputes Suspension Over Extortion Allegations
item.description: Dr Banji Olaleye, suspended Deputy Dean of Student Affairs at Federal University Oye-Ekiti (FUOYE), claims his removal was orchestrated before any investigation into extortion allegations. The univers...

item.title: Kenya's State Repression and Youth Protests in 2026
item.description: The article analyzes Kenya's political landscape in 2026, focusing on the government's heavy-handed response to youth-led protests. It highlights how the state has shifted from reactive crowd control ...

item.title: News Corp Australia blocks access for non-crawler users
item.description: The article details a technical barrier implemented by News Corp Australia to manage crawler bot traffic on their websites. Users encountering a block message are instructed to troubleshoot by disabli...

item.title: Ongoing Disputes in the West Philippine Sea Despite Legal Victory
item.description: A decade after the Philippines secured its historic arbitral victory against China over South China Sea claims, fishermen and activists continue to confront maritime disputes. In Subic Bay, fisherfolk...

item.title: PFIPC Scandal Unfolds as Investigation Reveals Fake Agency's Operations
item.description: Documents show the disowned Director-General of the disputed Presidential Foreign Intervention Promotion Council (PFIPC), Adeniyi Adeyemi, manipulated government agencies to secure CBN accounts and op...

item.title: Granit Xhaka's Leadership Journey: From Childhood Sacrifices to World Cup Leadership
item.description: Switzerland captain Granit Xhaka reflects on his formative years, highlighting how early responsibility and his parents' sacrifices shaped his leadership on the World Cup stage. Born in Switzerland to...

item.title: White Supremacist March in Washington, D.C.
item.description: The article discusses a white supremacist march in Washington, D.C., which has sparked debates about the state of democracy in the United States. The event, organized by far-right groups, drew signifi...

item.title: BIR Launches One-Time Tax Abatement Program for Micro Taxpayers
item.description: The Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) has introduced Revenue Regulations No. 4-2026, a one-time tax abatement program aimed at helping eligible micro taxpayers resolve unpaid tax obligations. This init...

item.title: Metro Vancouver Regional Parks Workers Stage Indefinite Strike Over Contract Deadlock
item.description: Over 100 workers with Metro Vancouver's regional parks have walked off the job indefinitely, citing frustration over stalled negotiations for 18 months. The union, Greater Vancouver Regional District ...

item.title: Ronaldo Reflects on Age, Criticism, and Legacy at World Cup
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo, 41, addressed media and fans ahead of Portugal's World Cup clash with Spain, acknowledging his decline in physical ability but emphasizing his mental resilience. The five-time Ballo...
